icd10Descriptions.M23.201: Derangement of unspecified lateral meniscus, right knee
icd10Descriptions.M23.211: Derangement of anterior horn lateral meniscus, right knee
icd10Descriptions.M23.221: Derangement of posterior horn lateral meniscus, right knee
icd10Descriptions.M23.311: Other meniscus derangements, medial meniscus, right knee
icd10Descriptions.S83.201A: Unspecified tear of lateral meniscus, right knee, initial
icd10Descriptions.S83.211A: Bucket-handle tear of lateral meniscus, right knee, initial

criteria.description: Failed conservative treatment: minimum 6 weeks of physical therapy, NSAIDs, and activity modification
Mechanical symptoms present: locking, catching, giving way, or inability to fully extend/flex
MRI findings consistent with meniscal tear (signal abnormality extending to articular surface)
Patient age under 65 OR if over 65, evidence that symptoms are primarily mechanical (not degenerative arthritis)
BMI under 40 OR if BMI over 40, documented weight management program participation
Surgeon is board-certified orthopedic surgeon with arthroscopy privileges

urgentBypass: Locked knee with inability to extend — authorize expedited review within 24 hours.
denialReasons: Insufficient conservative therapy (less than 6 weeks PT documented)
No mechanical symptoms — isolated pain without locking/catching
No MRI or MRI negative for meniscal pathology
Age >65 with primary osteoarthritis and no mechanical symptoms (arthroscopy not beneficial for degenerative disease)
